channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Dead As Disco Preview: Please Don’t Stop The Music
item.content:encoded:
Dead as Disco is self-described by the developers as ‘martial arts meets music video,’ and I’m not sure I could describe the game in a better way myself.

After an hour with a preview build—featuring two story missions and an endless mode—it’s clear the beat-based beat ’em up has real potential. Timing your attacks to the beat of the music feels immediately satisfying, blending performance and combat into one stylish loop.

It’s a familiar idea in some ways, especially with music-driven games making a comeback, but Dead As Disco leans heavily into its brawler roots. What stands out most is just how confident it feels visually and mechanically, even if some elements still need refinement.

Flashy, Fluid & Full Of Personality

The game’s presentation is easily its biggest strength. Its neon-drenched world is packed with personality, with levels ranging from subway tracks to surreal environments like planetary rings. Animations are slick and fluid, and when combat clicks, it looks fantastic in motion.

However, that fluidity comes at the cost of variety. Over time, moves begin to repeat, which slightly dulls the spectacle—there’s only so many times I want to counter-backhand someone. The environments themselves are creative, but transitions between them can feel abrupt.

That said, small shifts—like a brief side-scrolling section—hint at a surprising amount of variety beneath the surface.

Encounters themselves also benefit from environmental interaction, particularly in levels like the subway, where moving trains add an extra layer of awareness to combat. It’s these moments where Dead As Disco feels most inventive, blending its stylish presentation with gameplay ideas that could evolve into something truly memorable with more polish.

When The Beat Drops, So Do The Hits

Fighting to the beat is where Dead As Disco shines. When you’re in sync with the music, chaining attacks feels great, and the soundtrack itself is a highlight. Each boss even has its own dedicated song, ranging from heavy rock to punk pop, showcasing the diversity and fluidity of combat regardless of the track it’s backed by.

The ability to import your own music is a standout idea, and I soon found myself duking it out to the beats of Muse, Nothing But Thieves, and The Warning. That being said, it does require some effort to calibrate properly, and do people download music anymore?

It’s a fun gimmick, but probably not one I’d find myself enamoured by, especially when the base soundtrack does a killer job on its own.

The other side of the Dead as Disco coin is combat. While fun, it feels a little too easy in its current state, at least for my liking. Enemies can be slow to engage, and boss fights, while visually impressive, tend to drag and lack impact. There’s a noticeable absence of weight behind attacks, which holds the system back slightly.

That said, there’s clear replayability baked into the experience. Chasing higher scores, experimenting with different tracks, and refining your timing all add to the long-term appeal. If the full release can tighten the combat challenge and improve pacing, the core loop here has the potential to be incredibly addictive.

Dead As Disco Preview Round-Up

Dead As Disco is a stylish and promising rhythm-brawler with a fantastic core idea and striking presentation. While it currently lacks challenge and some combat impact, fighting to the beat has never felt so fluid, making it one to watch ahead of its Early Access release.

Will you be adding Dead as Disco to your Steam wishlist? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For some Insider Gaming reviews, check out the following:

  • Marathon
  • Super Meat Boy 3D
  • Crimson Desert

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: Don’t Starve Elsewhere Announced During Triple-I Initiative
item.content:encoded:
Another entry into the Don’t Starve franchise has been revealed, as Don’t Starve Elsewhere receives an official announcement.

The official news comes from the Triple-I Initiative 2026, with the gaming showcase’s third iteration featuring 40 games, with eight exclusive world premieres—Don’t Starve Elsewhere being one of them!

Check out the trailer below:

Don’t Starve Elsewhere Officially Announced

The Don’t Starve franchise is a pillar of the survival-crafting genre, and it looks like the series is getting a serious upgrade with Don’t Starve Elsewhere. The visuals have been hugely improved, all whilst maintaining its unique and striking art style.

Fans of the original should be especially excited, as the franchise’s winning formula remains untouched. You’ll be exploring incredibly harsh environments, gathering resources and building bases, all whilst everything is seemingly out to get you. It’s your task to endure the horrors of the world around you and do what it takes to survive.

Thankfully, you won’t have to do it alone, as Don’t Starve Elsewhere will feature fully fledged multiplayer, just like Don’t Starve Together. However, that will also mean more mouths to feed, so make sure your friends pull their weight in your bid for survival!

Are you happy to see a new Don’t Starve announcement? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more announcements, check out everything revealed at the Triple I Initiative Showcase.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Final Sentence Gets Sudden Shadow Drop During Triple I Initiative
item.content:encoded:
Indie games are where the imagination runs wild for new ideas. Final Sentence is the epitome of this, as it’s a typewriting battle royale game with elements of strategy, survival, and horror thrown in for good measure, and Final Sentence has been Shadow Dropped, meaning it’s out now.

Insider Gaming boldly proclaimed Final Sentence as the first game that game journalists are guaranteed to be good at! Is this strictly true? Well, everyone can find out right now, as the Triple I Initiative treated everyone with a Shadow Drop for Final Sentence.

Final Sentence Shadow Drop Announced

Final Sentence sees you wake up at a table with a typewriter in front of you. The mission is to accurately and quickly type out the prompts in front of you.

The game’s testing period is over, and there’s been nothing but praise for Final Sentence, including from IG. As a battle royale, this means you do need to be fast and efficient to make sure you’re not eliminated.

A ranking system will help to put you into more appropriate matches, so you’re not coming up against an 80 WPM (words per minute) demon that you shouldn’t be within a million miles of.

Here’s the Final Sentence Shadow Drop trailer for you to watch and get a taster of.

Have you seen anything like Final Sentence before? Do indie games need more attention and love? Insider Gaming is honoring this level of gaming with the upcoming Insider Gaming Showcase. To keep up with this and everything going on in general, consider joining the free Insider Gaming Discord!

Also, read about an Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake announcement being planned for next week for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Neverway Release Window Announced, Prologue Available Now
item.content:encoded:
Neverway, the highly anticipated life-simulation RPG, is officially set for launch on PC and Nintendo Switch later this year, but you can try out the game right now.

The Coldblood Inc. title puts players in control of Fiona, who quits her dead-end job and starts over on a farm, where she becomes the immortal herald of a dead god.

Neverway caught our eye ahead of the turn of the year, with 2026 set to be a big one for life simulation fans, and while the release window is still a ways off, a prologue is available to play immediately.

Neverway Releases October 2026, Prologue Available Now

Revealed during the Triple I Initiative showcase, Neverway has an official release window for PC and Nintendo Switch of October 2026.

While that allows fans to start a countdown to the full release, the other big news was the release of the prologue, which is available to play right now on Steam. Check out the trailer below.

Co-directed by the pixel artist of Celeste, Neverway is described as a blend of horror, life sim, and action RPG. Players must keep their lives from falling apart while searching for Neverway, a nightmare reality slowly leaking into yours.

The game includes over 10 players to meet or romance, each with unique routines and storylines, and each bond unlocks buffs and combat abilities to make your journey a little easier.

Alongside top-down, fast-paced combat, Neverway allows for crafting of items, farming, and decorating of the homestead, with time progressing in three blocks, where players choose to advance: morning, afternoon, and evening.

Are you excited for the release of Neverway? Share your thoughts on the announcements in the Insider Gaming Discord!

For more news, take a look at the new improvements to Xbox Achievements, and check out developers accusing Gunzilla Games of not paying salaries for months.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Risk of Rain 2: Hallowed Concepts DLC Announced
item.content:encoded:
Over six years after it was first released, Risk of Rain 2 is getting a brand new DLC.

Announced as part of the Triple I Initiative showcase on Thursday, Risk of Rain 2: Hallowed Concepts is a new expansion that “takes players deeper into the dark, cult-like world of N’Kuhana.”

In the new expansion from developers Gearbox Software, players have to fight through and explore new areas of Petrichor V. On their journey, players will “face horrific new enemies, and uncover powers never before possible in Risk of Rain 2.”

A release date for the Hallowed Concepts DLC wasn’t announced, but the developers say that it’s “coming soon”. Full details on what players can expect will also be announced at some point prior to launch.

Risk of Rain 2 first launched in early access in 2019 for PC and console. It was a follow-up to 2013’s Risk of Rain, but takes the series in a completely new direction.

Whereas the original Risk of Rain was a 2D roguelike platformer, Risk of Rain 2 is a co-op third-person shooter with roguelike elements. The game was first developed by Hopoo Games, but when the game was acquired by Gearbox in 2022, Hopoo stopped working on the game entirely.

In 2024, it was announced that the company’s founders, along with other employees, had begun working on game development for Valve. Since then, the studio itself has been silent.

Are you going to check out the Risk of Rain 2: Hallowed Concepts DLC when it launches? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about how Backyard Sports was brought back to life by a former elementary school teacher. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.title: TOO DEEP TO QUIT Unveiled During Triple I Initiative
item.content:encoded:
Get ready to go spelunking with your friends, as TOO DEEP TO QUIT, a game focused on puppets exploring hidden temples in search of rare treasures, has been officially announced. It’s set for release in 2026.

The official news comes from the Triple I Initiative 2026, with the gaming showcase’s third iteration featuring 40 games, with eight exclusive world premieres—this being one of them!

Check out the trailer here:

TOO DEEP TO QUIT Releasing In 2026

There’s been a boom in the indie multiplayer scene over the past few years, and it looks like TOO DEEP TO QUIT is about to add another must-play title to the list. You and up to three friends will be exploring procedural dungeons as puppets, all in the name of treasure!

However, don’t expect the journey to be easy, as you’ll come across a variety of dangers during your exploration. From enemies like snakes and spiders to hidden traps like spike pits and arrow walls, there’s plenty to make you watch your step.

It’s all tied together in a tidy knot thanks to its physics-based gameplay. Expect these puppets to ragdoll their way to the deepest depths of the dungeons, all whilst using a variety of tools to help navigate the countless hazards.

No specific release date has been revealed for TOO DEEP TO QUIT, but it’s slated for release sometime in 2026. It looks like one to keep an eye on, especially if you enjoyed the likes of PEAK and R.E.P.O.

What do you think of TOO DEEP TO QUIT’s reveal? Will you be giving it a go? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more announcements, check out everything revealed at the Triple I Initiative Showcase.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Alabaster Dawn Early Access Release Date Announced
item.content:encoded:
Alabaster Dawn’s Early Access release date has been confirmed, with players able to dive into the game in just a few weeks.

Radical Fish Games announced the Early Access release of their new title, following on from their previous game, CrossCode, which features puzzles to solve, secrets to discover, and a variety of locations to explore.

Alabaster Dawn Early Access Releases May 7

The Triple I Initiative showcased that Alabaster Dawn will be released in Early Access from May 7, giving players worldwide an opportunity to dive headfirst into the title, following the demo released last year.

Alabaster Dawn has seen development move to WebGL to support 3D graphics, and the entire CrossCode codebase has been rewritten in TypeScript to simplify console ports. Now, the Early Access launch is on the horizon.

Check out the trailer for the Early Access launch below.

Alabaster Dawn features a unique 2.5D art style, combining pixel art with 3D perspective, and a deep combat system that includes eight unique weapons. There will also be a large environment that changes and develops as the game progresses.

Initially, the developers set the title will release into Early Access on PC only, but there are plans a console launch for a later date. Additionally, additional updates will occur throughout development in Early Access to “bring the game to its best possible state.”

Radical Fish Games plans the title to be massive, with 30-60 hours of playtime, seven unique areas to explore, and a combat system inspired by the likes of Devil May Cry and Kingdom Hearts.

Are you excited for the Early Access launch of Alabaster Dawn? Share your thoughts on the game in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at a major Pokémon Champions bug that blocks transfers from Pokémon Home, and check out a new Steam Workshop update that improves browsing and performance.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: The Lift Coming To PlayStation 5 & Xbox Series X|S
item.content:encoded:
Good news for console fans, as The Lift is officially coming to both the PlayStation 5 and the Xbox Series X|S. In this eerie, narrative experience, you’ll be doing your best work as a handyman while renovating a mysterious, abandoned research facility.

The official news comes from the Triple I Initiative 2026, with the gaming showcase’s third iteration featuring 40 games, with eight exclusive world premieres.

Check out the trailer below:

The Lift Coming To Consoles

It’s easy to call The Lift a handyman simulator, but the experience runs far deeper than that. The developers boast a story-driven experience, where you’ll be exploring a highly interactive world inspired by Soviet science fiction. Figure out what mysterious incident took place in this abandoned facility, and understand why it’s long been abandoned.

That being said, the handyman gameplay is at the core of The Lift, though it’s undergone a surreal twist. Instead of putting up shelves and painting walls, you’ll be vacuuming reality-distorting anomalies and collecting fuel from robotic bees. As if that wasn’t crazy enough, try repairing a sentient reactor!

There’s a lot to uncover in The Lift, and after its original reveal for PC, it’s now officially coming to consoles, with the PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S it’s future homes. No release date has been confirmed just yet, but the confirmation of its arrival is exciting enough as is.

Are you excited to pick up The Lift on consoles? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more announcements, check out everything revealed at the Triple I Initiative Showcase.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Away Team Announces Co-Op, Sign Ups Open For First Playtest
item.content:encoded:
Away Team, the next adventure in the Oxygen Not Included series, has announced co-op will be featured in the title, while sign ups are now open for the first playtest.

Set to release later this year, Away Team sees players mistakenly shipped off on a research rocket, encountering a strange new world where you must take care of the simulation to survive and thrive.

The good news is, you won’t have to do it alone.

Multiplayer Announced For Away Team

As part of the Triple I Initiative showcase, co-op functionality for Away Team has been announced, a major step for the Oxygen Not Included series after years of community requests.

Fans of the series have previously had to turn to mods to experience multiplayer, but that won’t be required when Away Team launches later this year, as Klei Entertainment announced multiplayer is now in development.

Check out the trailer shown at the Triple I Initiative showcase below.

Alongside the announcement of multiplayer, it was also announced that sign-ups are now open to be a part of Away Team’s first single-player playtest on Steam.

While multiplayer functionality won’t be part of that playtest, it will provide select players with the first opportunity to try out Away Team, ahead of the eagerly anticipated launch later this year.

Are you excited by the confirmation of multiplayer coming to Away Team? Share your thoughts on the news in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more news, read about the delay to 007 First Light’s Nintendo Switch 2 launch, and check out the latest No Man’s Sky update.
